label: Encoder–decoder transformers

node.label: Encoder–decoder transformers
node.definition: An encoder–decoder transformer builds a bidirectional source representation and generates a target sequence through cross-attention and causal decoding.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Encoder–decoder transformers is modeled as depending on Transformers, Cross-attention & co-attention and feeding downstream nodes such as Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding.
examples: atlas downstream link to Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Transformers, Cross-attention & co-attention.

dependencies.rationale: Encoder–decoder transformers extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
Encoder–decoder transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Cross-attention & co-attention.
Encoder–decoder transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Causal, masked & structured masking.
Encoder–decoder transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Autoregressive decoding.
